title: chenopodium × hungaricum
intro: Chenopodium × hungaricum, also known as Hungarian sorrel or Hungarian goosefoot, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Chenopodium ambrosioides and Chenopodium album. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Chenopodium × hungaricum include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine, although its efficacy and safety may vary depending on the preparation and individual tolerance.
